title: Life Is a Tarantino Movie + Drive-Thru Mini Market Talk with Dad
html:
TL;DR

  • Time-stamped 10:10pm on May 7, 2020 — late-night reflection in the depths of the COVID quarantine. Opens with the line: "My life is a movie directed by Tarantino" — fragmented violence-and-quiet-moments framing of his daily reality.
  • Same day's narrative arc: "After fights in morning, I came home to give Lola food. We talked about business, the spur of evolving, growth potentials. I will continue to push the drive thru mini market." The morning fights and the evening business-talk-with-dad sit in the same paragraph — the dad as source and as wound pattern in compressed form.
  • The drive-thru mini market is a teen Yahir business idea — same value-investing-applied-to-building instinct that runs through the pandemic watchlist and the FB Marketplace fleet. First explicit articulation of a brick-and-mortar concept (versus the digital reselling).
  • Dad-quote (paraphrased): "I came home frustrated and beaten down on my luck and I acquired an urge or sense of ambition and hope for the future thanks to you." A rare positive dad-attribution — the dad wisdom genre's inverse pole, where the son is the wisdom-source for the father.
  • Captured one week after the 1987 crash dad question dream note and the night after the tribal fight dream. May 7–8, 2020 is a high-density emotional window.

Key quotes

"My life is a movie directed by Tarantino."

"I came home frustrated and beaten down on my luck and I acquired an urge or sense of ambition and hope for the future thanks to you." — dad, paraphrased

Open questions

  • Drive-thru mini market — did this concept ever get past the talk stage? No subsequent execution-note in the corpus so far. Worth tracing forward.
  • Tarantino comparison — the only film-directed-by analogy in the corpus this early. Genre worth watching for.

Connections
